This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kishiwada City, Kaizuka City.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:new pages]][[ja:九十九王子 (Kishiwada City, Kaizuka City)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ** {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}}''' → '''{{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}}''' → '''{{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}}''' → '''{{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}}''' → {{ill|Tsuruhara Ōji|ja|鶴原王子}}

This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ** {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}}''' → '''{{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}}''' → '''{{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}}''' → '''{{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}}''' → {{ill|Tsuruhara Ōji|ja|鶴原王子}}

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kishiwada City, Kaizuka City.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:new pages]][[ja:九十九王子 (Kishiwada City, Kaizuka City)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ** {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}}''' → '''{{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}}''' → '''{{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}}''' → '''{{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}}''' → {{ill|Tsuruhara Ōji|ja|鶴原王子}}

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kishiwada City, Kaizuka City.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:new pages]][[ja:九十九王子 (Kishiwada City, Kaizuka City)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ** {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kishiwada City, Kaizuka City)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kishiwada City, Kaizuka City)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kishiwada City, Kaizuka City)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kishiwada City, Kaizuka City)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:new pages]][[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ** {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}
== References == * {{Cite book |author=Ritsu Nishi |title=Kumano Kōdō Michishirube: Kumano Kujūku Ōji Genjō Tōsaro |year=1987 |publisher=Arao Seibundō}} [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kaizuka, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:神社関連テンプレート|くしゆくおうし]] [[Category:九十九王子|-てんふれえと]] [[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]][[zh:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqq]]

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:new pages]][[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ** {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ** {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ** {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ** {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ** {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ** {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}
== References == * {{Cite book |author=Ritsu Nishi |title=Kumano Kōdō Michishirube: Kumano Kujūku Ōji Genjō Tōsaro |year=1987 |publisher=Arao Seibundō}} [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[en:List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kaizuka, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:神社関連テンプレート|くしゆくおうし]] [[Category:九十九王子|-てんふれえと]] [[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]][[zh:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[en:List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] {{citation needed|bottom}}[[Category:sqq]]

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.

This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}

revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}
== References == * {{Cite book |author=Ritsu Nishi |title=Kumano Kōdō Michishirube: Kumano Kujūku Ōji Genjō Tōsaro |year=1987 |publisher=Arao Seibundō}} [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[en:List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kaizuka, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:神社関連テンプレート|くしゆくおうし]] [[Category:九十九王子|-てんふれえと]] [[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]][[zh:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[en:List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] {{citation needed|bottom}}[[Category:sqq]]

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)-qq]] [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka)|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka)|ja|熊野神社}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (泉佐野市)|ja|九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}
