revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
<!--Kaizuka)--> [[ja:九十九王子 (Kaizuka)]] ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka|ja|熊野神社}} site. == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Izumisano City)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}'''

The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka|ja|熊野神社}} site. == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=Kaizuka)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Izumisano City)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子}}'''

revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=draft}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
<!--Kaizuka)-->
==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|12=draft}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市|12=draft}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=draft}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=draft}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=draft}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|12=draft}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=draft}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線|12=draft}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=draft}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=draft}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=draft}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=draft}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=draft}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=draft}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=draft}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=draft}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=draft}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka|ja|熊野神社|12=draft}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子|12=draft}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=Kaizuka)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Izumisano City)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}'''
== References == * {{Cite book |author=Ritsu Nishi |title=Kumano Kōdō Michishirube: Kumano Kujūku Ōji Genjō Tōsaro |year=1987 |publisher=Arao Seibundō}}
<!--Kaizuka)--> <!--Kaizuka)-->
<!--Kaizuka)--> <!--Kaizuka)--> {{citation needed|bottom}}

=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=draft}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線|12=draft}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=draft}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=draft}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=draft}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=draft}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=draft}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=draft}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=draft}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=draft}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=draft}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka|ja|熊野神社|12=draft}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子|12=draft}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=Kaizuka)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Izumisano City)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}'''

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=draft}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
<!--Kaizuka)-->
==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|12=draft}} | Established = | Map = }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|ja|岸和田市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|ja|貝塚市|12=draft}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}}.
== Overview of Kujūku Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}}}
The Kujūku Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=draft}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=draft}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=draft}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|ja|承久の乱|12=draft}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Kujūku Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Kujūku Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=draft}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|ja|久米田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線|12=draft}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=draft}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=draft}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=draft}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=draft}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=draft}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=draft}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=draft}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=draft}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=draft}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka|ja|熊野神社|12=draft}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子|12=draft}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=Kaizuka)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Izumisano City)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}'''
== References == * {{Cite book |author=Ritsu Nishi |title=Kumano Kōdō Michishirube: Kumano Kujūku Ōji Genjō Tōsaro |year=1987 |publisher=Arao Seibundō}}

=== {{ill|Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=draft}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=draft}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=draft}}.
=== {{ill|Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=draft}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=draft}} or the former {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka|ja|熊野神社|12=draft}} site.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Inokuchi Ōji|ja|井ノ口王子|12=draft}} → '''{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|fr|Yagi-jinja|lt=Ikeda Ōji|error=Kaizuka)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Asokawa Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Kogi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → '''{{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}''' → {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Izumisano City)|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=List of Kujūku Ōji (Kaizuka)|en|lt=Tsuruhara Ōji|error=九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)|oji=王子|12=draft}}'''

revision.text:
本項目では、[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]および[[貝塚市]]内に所在する'''[[九十九王子]]'''（くじゅうくおうじ）について述べる。
== 九十九王子とは == {{Main|九十九王子}}
九十九王子（くじゅうくおうじ）とは、[[熊野古道]]、特に[[紀伊路]]沿いに在する[[神社]]のうち、主に[[12世紀]]から[[13世紀]]にかけて、皇族・貴人の熊野詣に際して先達をつとめた熊野修験の手で急速に組織された一群の神社をいい、参詣者の守護が祈願された。
しかしながら、[[承久]]3年（[[1221年]]）の[[承久の乱]]以降、[[京都|京]]からの熊野詣が下火になり、そのルートであった紀伊路が衰退するとともに、荒廃と退転がすすんだ。[[室町時代]]以降、熊野詣がかつてのような卓越した地位を失うにつれ、この傾向はいっそう進み、[[江戸時代|近世]][[紀州藩]]の手による顕彰も行なわれたものの、勢いをとどめるまでには至らなかった。さらに、[[明治]]以降の[[神道]]の[[国家神道]]化とそれに伴う合祀、市街化による廃絶などにより、旧社地が失われたり、比定地が不明になったものも多い。
本記事では、これら九十九王子のうち、比定地が大阪府岸和田市および貝塚市内にある王子を扱う。
== 九十九王子 == 岸和田市内の九十九王子は1社、貝塚市内の九十九王子は3社。
=== 池田王子 === ''' 池田王子'''は、[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[阪和線]][[久米田駅]]に近い私有地（岸和田市下池田）内に比定される。かつては、畑の中に土が舞台上に盛り上げられた草舞台と呼ばれていた場所があり、王子跡碑があった。しかし現在では、その痕跡も失われ、畑と駐車場になっている。
『和泉誌』には「今云熊野権現」、『泉州誌』には「積川王子、或云池田王子、余按、昔此辺、積川王子社領地」との記述があり、積川王子の別名から、[[積川神社]]（岸和田市積川町）との関連性が示唆されている。明治期の神社合祀に際して、[[夜疑神社]]（岸和田市中井町2-7-1）に合祀され廃絶した。
* 最寄り交通機関 - [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[阪和線]][[久米田駅]]下車後、徒歩
=== 麻生川王子 === '''麻生川王子'''（あそかわおうじ）は、[[1909年]]（[[明治42年]]）、阿里莫神社（貝塚市久保165番地）に合祀され廃絶。浅宇川王子、鹿王子との別表記あり。
半田集落の氏神として崇敬されていた神社。現在、比定地には工作機器メーカーの工場があり、その近くに全くに偶然ながら、末広大神の鎮守社が鎮座している。近隣には、[[熊野街道]]の一里塚跡があり、それを記念する道標が立てられている。 * 所在地 貝塚市半田
=== 鞍持王子 === '''鞍持王子'''（くらもちおうじ）は、[[1907年]]（[[明治40年]]）、南近義神社（みなみこぎじんじゃ、貝塚市王子）に合祀され廃絶。現在、比定地には鳥居と小社祠が遺されている。
『貝塚市史』『和泉誌』などに言及があり、橋本村大字王子原宮にあった旨が記述されているが、異説によれば、正福寺（貝塚市地蔵堂）の東横が社地であるともいう。
* 所在地 貝塚市王子
=== 近木王子 === '''近木王子'''（こぎおうじ）は明治末に南近義神社に合祀され廃社となった。胡木新王子との別表記あり。
善正寺（貝塚市地蔵堂）の東側が旧社地に比定されるが、吉祥園寺（貝塚市王子）の南の王子村字権現にあった熊野神社（現在廃社）を旧社地とする説もある。 * 所在地 貝塚市地蔵堂
== 参考文献 == 西 律、1987、『熊野古道みちしるべ - 熊野九十九王子現状踏査録』、荒尾成文堂（みなもと選書1）
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] ** （[[井ノ口王子]]） - '''池田王子''' - '''麻生川王子''' - '''近木王子''' - '''鞍持王子''' - （[[九十九王子 (泉佐野市)#鶴原王子|鶴原王子]]）
{{九十九王子}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしゆうくおうし}} [[Category:九十九王子|きしわたしかいつかし]] [[Category:大阪府の神社|くしゆうくおうしきしわたしかいつかし]] [[Category:貝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11370044]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]].
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near [[:en:Kumeda Station|Kumeda Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]], the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|qq=|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|qq=|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|qq=|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka]] site.

== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near [[:en:Kumeda Station|Kumeda Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]], the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|qq=|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|qq=|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|qq=|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka]] site.

This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]].
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near [[:en:Kumeda Station|Kumeda Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]], the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|qq=|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|qq=|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|qq=|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka]] site.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370044]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]].
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near [[:en:Kumeda Station|Kumeda Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]], the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka]] site.

==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]].
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near [[:en:Kumeda Station|Kumeda Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]], the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka]] site.

This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]].
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near [[:en:Kumeda Station|Kumeda Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]], the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka]] site.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370044]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]].
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near [[:en:Kumeda Station|Kumeda Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]], the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka]] site.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370044]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] and [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]], which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]].
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near [[:en:Kumeda Station|Kumeda Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]], the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka]] site.

== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]], especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]] in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near [[:en:Kumeda Station|Kumeda Station]] on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]], the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka]] site.
