<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11370044]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}}.
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka}} site.

This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--><!--Kaizuka)--><!--Kaizuka)--><!--Kaizuka)--><!--Kaizuka)--><!--Kaizuka)--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11370044}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Kaizuka, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Lists of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]]
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}}.
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka}} site.

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}}.
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka}} site.

== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka}} site.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]]
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}}.
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Sanzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka}} site.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]]
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}}.
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040|1=Kumano Shugendo}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Three Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain|1=Kishū Domain}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station|1=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Q11545584|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka|1=Kumano shrine}} site.

This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}}.
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040|1=Kumano Shugendo}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Three Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain|1=Kishū Domain}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station|1=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Q11545584|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka|1=Kumano shrine}} site.

== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493040|1=Kumano Shugendo}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Three Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain|1=Kishū Domain}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station|1=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Q11545584|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64029230}} or the former {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka|1=Kumano shrine}} site.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]]
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}}.
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}

These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Three Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10403700}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain|1=Kishū Domain}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.

=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station|1=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--><!--Kaizuka)--><!--Kaizuka)--><!--Kaizuka)--><!--Kaizuka)--><!--Kaizuka)--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11370044}} [[Category:History of Kaizuka, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Lists of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)]]
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|qid=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|qid=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|qid=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|qid=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}}.
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493040|1=Kumano Shugendo}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Three Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10403700}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|qid=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|qid=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain|1=Kishū Domain}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|qid=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station|1=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|qid=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Q11545584|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q64029230}} or the former {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|qid=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka|1=Kumano shrine}} site.

revision.text:
This is a list of some of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. This article covers the shrines in the regions of Kaizuka.
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | image = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|qid=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|qid=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = | Map = | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} }}
This article covers the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''' shrines located within {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|qid=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|qid=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, which were part of the historic pilgrimage route to {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}}.
== Overview of Ninety-Nine Ōji == {{Main|{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}}}
The Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines refer to a group of sacred sites along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}}, especially the {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} route, established during the 12th and 13th centuries by {{ill|Kumano Shugendō|ja|熊野修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493040|1=Kumano Shugendo}} practitioners. These shrines served to protect pilgrims, including members of the imperial family and nobility, on their journeys to the sacred sites of Kumano.
Following the decline of the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Three Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10403700}} after the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|qid=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}} in 1221, many of these shrines fell into disrepair. Despite some restoration efforts during the Edo period by the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|qid=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain|1=Kishū Domain}}, many shrines were lost due to urbanization and the State Shinto reforms of the Meiji era.
This article focuses on the Ninety-Nine Ōji shrines identified within Kishiwada and Kaizuka.
== Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines == There is one known Ōji shrine in Kishiwada and three in Kaizuka.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Ikeda Ōji|ja|池田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Located near {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|qid=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station|1=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|qid=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}, the former site of Ikeda Ōji is now private land in Shimo-Ikeda, Kishiwada. The shrine was believed to be associated with {{ill|Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597137}} and was merged into {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} during the Meiji period.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Asokawa Ōji|ja|麻生川王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asokawa Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Aso River Ōji, it was merged into {{ill|Arimaku Shrine|ja|阿里莫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1909. The shrine was venerated by the Handa community, and although its original location is now occupied by a factory, nearby landmarks commemorate its historical significance.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kuramochi Ōji|ja|鞍持王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramochi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Merged into {{ill|Minami Kogi Shrine|ja|南近義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Kogi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kaizuka in 1907, the former shrine site still has a small torii gate and shrine structure. Some historical records suggest alternative locations, including near {{ill|Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|ja|正福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōfuku-ji (Kaizuka|1=Q11545584|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545584}}.
=== {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kaizuka)|lt=Kogi Ōji|ja|近木王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (岸和田市・貝塚市)}} === Also known as Kogi Shin Ōji, it was merged into Minami Kogi Shrine in the late Meiji era. The former shrine site is debated, with possible locations near {{ill|Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|ja|善正寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenshō-ji (Kaizuka|1=Zenshō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q64029230}} or the former {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|qid=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine (Kaizuka|1=Kumano shrine}} site.
